original: The temple of Apollo at Daphne, outside of Antioch, is destroyed in a mysterious fire
Japanese Emperor Kanmu relocates the capital from Nara to Heian-kyo (now Kyoto)
Ex-emperor Hanazono (95th Emperor of Japan) becomes a Zen priest
The 1383-85 Crisis in Portugal: A period of civil war and disorder begins after King Fernando dies without a male heir to the Portuguese throne
Foundation of Aguascalientes (one of 32 Federal Entities of Mexico)
Battle of Liaoluo Bay: Dutch East India Company defeated by Chinese Ming naval forces in southern Fujian sea
Scilly Naval Disaster: shipwreck of four Royal navy warships and loss of 1400 men during a storm off the Scilly Isles - will prompt 1714 British Act of Parliament competition to find longitude at sea
Great Alliance occupies Rijsel
Princeton University (NJ) received its charter
Andre-Jacques Garnerin makes 1st parachute descent from a balloon in Paris
Russia leaves second anti-French Coalition
German chemist Friedrich Wöhler is the first to isolate pure aluminium
Millerite Adventists wait for appearance of Jesus
1st telegraph line linking West & East coasts completed
Battle at Old Fort Wayne, Indian Territory
Confederate troops reconquer Cumperland Gap, Tennessee
Foundation of the National University of Colombia.
Jacques Offenbach's opera "Genevieve de Brabant" premieres in NYC
First telegraphic connection in Argentina.
Sons of American Revolution organizes
The Blantyre mining disaster in Scotland kills 207 miners. Those widows and orphans who were unable to support themselves were evicted by the mine owners and likely sent to the Poor House.
The first rugby match under floodlights takes place in Salford, between Broughton and Swinton.
Boston Symphony Orchestra gives its first concert
1st NY Horse Show held (Madison Sq Garden)
General Gordon receives letter of Mahdi
International Meridian Conference in Washington, D.C. adopts Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) worldwide and creates 24 international time zones with longitude zero at the Greenwich meridian
Sporting Life announces that both pennant winners will meet in 3 game series Oct 23-25 at Polo Grounds NYC to determine baseball champion
John Ward & several teammates secretly form Brotherhood of Prof Base Ball Players, 1st baseball union
David Belasco's "Heart of Maryland" premieres in NYC
In Paris an express train overruns a buffer stop, crosses more than 30 meters of concourse before plummeting through a window at Gare Montparnasse
British troops flee Dundee, Natal South Africa
Russian fleet shoots at British fishing ship
3000 blacks demonstrate & riot in Philadelphia
A run on Knickerbocker Trust Company stock leads to a nationwide run on US banks and the Panic of 1907
Dr. Crippen is convicted at the Old Bailey of poisoning his wife and was subsequently hanged at Pentonville Prison in London.
Explosion at Stag Canyon coal mine kills 263 workers, near Dawson, New Mexico, only 23 miners survived
Parsifal Place laid out in Bronx, NY, named after a knight in Wagner's opera
Toastmasters International is founded
China expels all Russian instructors & civil servants
ESL Robinson's "Far-Off Hills" premieres in Dublin
Phi Sigma Alpha fraternity is founded at the University of Puerto Rico, Rio Piedras Campus.
James H Scullin forms Australia government
1st concert of BBC Symphony Orchestra, at Queen's Hall, under Adrian Boult
Blake & Razaf's "Lew Leslie's Blackbirds of 1930" premieres in NYC
SC Genemuiden soccer team forms
Charles de Broqueville becomes premier of Belgium
George Kaufman & Edna Ferbers "Dinner at 8" premieres in NYC
Establishment of the rank of Marshal of the Soviet Union
1st commercial flight from mainland to Hawaii
Chester Carlson has 1st successful experiment in electrophotography, later sold to the Haloid Company and dubbed "Xerox copying"
NBC becomes first network to televise a pro football game; Brooklyn Dodgers beat Philadelphia Eagles, 23-14 at Brooklyn's Ebbets Field
1st ships of invasion fleet for Oran (Algeria) leave Scotland

Operation Flagpole: US Major General Mark Clark & Brigadier General Lyman Lemnitzer meet Vichy French Général Charles Mast secretly in small fishing village of Cherchell, Algeria to finalize plans for Allied Invasion of North Africa
David Diamond's 2nd Symphony premieres with the Boston Symphony Orchestra conducted by Serge Koussevitzky
WWII: Japanese Navy Admiral Takeo Kurita's fleet leaves North Borneo
2 British ships sink near Albania
First Arab–Israeli War: Israeli Navy sinks Egyptian Navy flagship 'King Farouk' using explosive-laden motorboat in the Mediterranean Sea near Gaza
200 killed in train derailment near Nowy Dwor Poland
Emile Zatopek runs world record 10,000m (29:21.2)
LA Rams beat Baltimore Colts 70-27
Earthquake hits Formosa, 100 killed
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Laos gains full independence from France
West Germany joins North Atlantic Treaty Organization
WWNY TV channel 7 in Carthage-Watertown, NY (CBS) begins broadcasting
A concrete girder weighing 200 tons kills 48 in Karachi, Pakistan.
Great Britain performs nuclear test at Maralinga, Australia
KJAC TV channel 4 in Port Arthur-Beaumont, TX (NBC) 1st broadcast
1st Japanese anime feature film in color "The Tale of the White Serpent" released, produced by Toei Doga Company
75,000 Flemings demand equal rights & Flemish language in Belgium
Pacific Science Center opens at Seattle Center
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
225,000 students boycot Chicago schools in Freedom Day protest
BAC One-Eleven prototype airliner crashes on October 22 in UK with the loss of all on board.
EMI rejects audition by "High Numbers"; they go on to become "The Who"
US performs underground nuclear test at Hattiesburg, Mississippi
USSR launches Luna 12 for orbit around Moon

Denny Hulme becomes first New Zealander to win the F1 World Drivers Championship with 3rd place in the Mexican Grand Prix at Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez; wins title by 5 points from team leader Jack Brabham
Ian Brayshaw (W Aus v Vic, Perth) takes all 10 cricket wkts
American swimmers sweep the medals in the women's 200m freestyle at the Mexico City Olympics; Debbie Meyer takes gold in Olympic record 2:10.5 ahead of teammates Jan Henne and Jane Barkman
Apollo 7 returns to Earth
KAPN-AM in Santa Barbara CA changes call letters to KDB-AM
USSR performs nuclear test
Israeli troops reconquer mountain Hermon
Security Council Resolution 338-cease fire to Yom Kippur War
Soviet spacecraft Venera 9 soft-lands on Venus, becoming the first lander to return images from the surface of another planet
Turkish diplomat shot to death in Vienna
World Football League disbands after the Week 12 of their second season
Red Dye No. 4 is banned by the US Food and Drug Administration after it is discovered that it causes tumors in the bladders of dogs. The dye is still used in Canada.
International Sun-Earth Explorers 1 & 2 launched into Earth orbit
"Laugh-In" comedienne Judy Carne is arrested at Gatwick Airport for drug possession
Grete Weitz runs female world record marathon (2:32:29.8)
"One Mo' Time" with Vernel Bagners premieres in NYC
Walt Disney World's 100-millionth guest
4th government of Martens forms in Belgium
New South Korean constitution comes into effect
Professional Air Traffic Controllers Organization decertified
Start of 1st-class game at Newcastle, NSW v Queensland
The founding congress of the Nepal Workers and Peasants Organisation faction led by Hareram Sharma and D.P. Singh begins.
The TGV railway service Paris-Lyon is inaugurated.
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved aspartame artificial sweetener for tabletop use following years of scrutiny
US national debt tops $1 trillion
USSR performs underground nuclear test
Gene Mauch resigns as manager of Angels
Two correctional officers are killed by inmates in Marion, Illinois. The incident inspired the Supermax model of prisons.
Future Pro Football HOF quarterback Ken Stabler retires after 17 seasons in the NFL with Oakland Raiders, Houston Oilers and New Orleans Saints
Paul McCartneyreleases "Give My Regards to Broad Street" soundtrack
WNBC-AM NYC helicopter crashes into Hudson River during a live report, killing traffic reporter Jane Dornacker
American composer John Adams's first opera, "Nixon in China", premieres at the Houston Grand Opera, in Houston, Texas
Nobel prize for literature awarded to Joseph Brodsky
Denver Nuggets beat Jugoplastika Split 135-129 in 3rd McDonald Open
Red Khmer occupies Pailin in Cambodia
General Motors announces 9 month loss of $US2.2 billion
Space Shuttle STS 52 (Columbia 13) launches into space
Russian cosmonaut Aleksandr Serebrov makes record 9th space walk
Statue ofSam Houstonunveiled in Texas
Tony Rominger bicycles world record time (53,832 km)
NY Yankee Bernie Williams hits record tying 7th post season HR, as Yanks tie record of 6th straight post season road win (en route to 8)
Compaq testifies Microsoft threaten to break Windows 95 agreement if they showcased a Netscape icon
Yahoo completes purchase of Four11
Maurice Papon, an official in the Vichy France government during World War II, is jailed for crimes against humanity.
Discovery and isolation of new wonder material Graphene announced in paper by Andre Geim and Konstantin Novoselov (Nobel Prize 2010). Incredibly they used scotch tape to peal layers off Graphite.
Tropical Storm Alpha forms in the Atlantic Basin, making the 2005 Atlantic Hurricane Season the most active Atlantic hurricane season on record with 22 named storms.
Panama Canal expansion proposal is approved by 77.8% of voters in a National referendum held in Panama

Spanish Renault driver Fernando Alonso finishes 2nd in season-ending Brazilian Grand Prix at Autódromo José Carlos Pace to retain Formula 1 World Drivers Championship; wins by 13 points fromMichael Schumacher
Google Play is launched, the official app store for the Android operating system
India launches its first unmanned lunar mission, Chandrayaan-1
Microsoft releases Windows 7
6 Italian scientists are convicted of manslaughter for their failure to predict the 2009 L'Aquila earthquake
Hurricane Sandy forms in the Western Caribbean Sea
"Lego Marvel Super Heroes" first released in North America for some formats including Xbox and Wii - Lego's best-selling video game
22 security force personal are killed in attacks on military checkpoints in Anbar Province, Iraq
37 Boko Harem Islamist militants are killed by air and ground strikes in Nigeria
AT&T buy Time Warner for $85.4 billion
"LA Times" publishes investigation with 38 woman accusing Hollywood writer-director James Toback of sexual harassment
Cameroon's President Paul Biya wins seventh term in office, extending his 36 years in office, in election marred by intimidation and low turnout
Legislation for Northern Ireland legalizing same-sex marriage and abortion comes into effect
UK parliament approves Brexit deal to leave the EU but rejects legislation to fast-track it to meet October 31 deadline
US drug company Biogen claims to have created 1st drug to slow advance of Alzheimer's disease called aducanumab
Goldman Sachs agrees to pay record $3 billion to end probe into its role in 1MDB corruption scandal to regulators in the US, UK, Hong Kong, Singapore and Malaysia
Australian city of Melbourne "world's most locked down city" exits its sixth lockdown after a total of 260 days
Italy forms new coalition government, choosing far-right Brothers of Italy (Fratelli d'Italia - FdI) leader Giorgia Meloni as Prime Minister, the 1st female to hold the office
Construction begins on the largest building ever built, when completed, the Mukaab skyscraper, cube shaped, 400 meters wide and 300 meters tall, in Saudi Arabia's capital Riyadh
UK and Germany sign the Trinity House Agreement; a "landmark defence treaty"
TsarPeter the Greattitles himself "Emperor of All Russia"
Duke of Wellingtonseizes Burgos, Spain
Sam Houstoninaugurated as 1st elected President of the Republic of Texas
Thomas Edisonperfects the carbonized cotton filament light bulb
New York's original Metropolitan Opera House has its grand opening with a performance ofCharles Gounod's opera "Faust"
Henry Fordbecomes President of Ford Motor Company
Ringling Brothers Greatest Show on Earth buysBarnum & Baileycircus

SuffragetteInez Milhollandcollapses during a speech in Los Angeles, California and dies weeks later; her last words are to US PresidentWoodrow Wilson“Mr. President, how long must women wait for liberty?”

Boxer J. Gordon Whitehead sucker punches magicianHarry Houdiniin the stomach in his dressing room at the Princess Theater in Montreal. The attack started or helped cause the appendicitis that would takeHoudini's life 9 days later.
Herbert Hooverspeaks of "American system of rugged individualism"
8th French government ofAristide Briandfalls
Film "Red Dust" directed byVictor Fleming, starringClark Gable,Jean HarlowandMary Astorpremieres
Italian boxerPrimo Carnerabeats Spanish challenger Paulino Uzcudun on points in Rome, Italy to retain the IBU heavyweight title
Notorious bank robberCharles "Pretty Boy" Floydis shot and killed by FBI agents in East Liverpool, Ohio
President of CzechoslovakiaEdvard Benešgoes into exile in London, in the aftermath of the Munich agreement and German annexation of Sudetenland
Maxwell Anderson's play "Candle in the Wind" premieres in NYC
"Now, Voyager" film directed by Irving Rapper, and starringBette Davis,Paul Henreidand Claude Rains, premieres in New York; wins 3 Academy Awards
France intercept Moroccan plane, arrestAhmed Ben Bella
Konrad Adenauerre-elected Chancellor of West Germany
JFKreceives Ugandan premier Milton Obote
French philosopher and authorJean-Paul Sartrerefuses the Nobel Prize for Literature, saying he doesn't want to be "Institutionalised"
Ice hockey legendBobby Orrscores his first career goal (vs Montreal Canadiens)
Joe DiMaggiois hired as executive VP of A's by Charlie Finley
Paul McCartneydenies greatly exaggerated rumors of his death
MLB New York Yankees trade outfielderBobby Murcerto San Francisco Giants for outfielderBobby Bonds
Rick Barry(San Francisco), begins then longest NBA free throw streak of 60
New York City Marathon:Grete Waitzof Norway wins in her race debut in world record 2:32:30;Bill Rodgerswins his third straight title in 2:12:12
PopeJohn Paul IIis inaugurated as Pope
Deposed Shah of IranMohammad Reza Pahlaviarrives in NYC for medical treatment
Elton Johnsells out Madison Square Garden, NYC, for a record 26th time
US Supreme Court JusticeSandra Day O'Connorsurvives breast cancer surgery

Over-competitive McLaren teammatesAlain Prost&Ayrton Sennafamously collide and skid into an escape road during Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka; Prost retires from race and claims 3rd Formula 1 World Drivers Championship
Wendy Wasserstein's play "The Sisters Rosensweig" premieres  off-Broadway in NYC
Larry Flyntsells Hustler to an under-age buyer in a non-zoned area of Cincinnati, Ohio
NHL superstarWayne Gretzky's wife Janet is knocked unconscious and gets 2 stitches after plexiglass falls on her watching a game
NHL goaltenderGrant Fuhrwins his 400th career game (vs Florida Panthers)

German Ferrari driverMichael Schumacherwins Malaysian Grand Prix at Sepang, his 4th straight race victory to finish season; 3rd, and 1st of 5 straight F1 World Drivers Championships; wins by 19 points from Mika Häkkinen
Former Uruguayan dictatorGregorio Álvarezsentenced to 25 years in prison for aggravated murder of 37 Uruguayan dissidents who had fled to Argentina
"Red" 4th studio album byTaylor Swiftis released (Billboard Album of the Year 2012/2013)
Kendrick Lamarreleases his concept album and second studio album "good kid, m.A.A.d city"
"Thor: The Dark World", directed by Alan Taylor, starringChris HemsworthandNatalie Portman, premieres in London
ActressSelma Blairreveals she has been diagnosed with multiple sclerosis
Australian Prime MinisterScott Morrisonmakes a public apology to victims of child sexual abuse in institutions
Pipe bomb sent toGeorge Soros' New York home address, first Democrat to receive series of pipe bombs in US
Chilean presidentSebastián Piñeraagrees to meet opposition leaders after 15 people killed in growing nationwide protests over living costs and inequality
Japanese EmperorNaruhitoalongside Empress Masako formally declares his ascension to the throne of the world's oldest monarchy
Russian PresidentVladimir Putinand Turkish PresidentRecep Tayyip Erdoğanagree deal to jointly control former Kurdish territory in Northern Syria
Top US diplomat in Ukraine, Bill Taylor, testifies PresidentDonald Trumptied aid to Ukraine to demands the country open an investigation into theBidenfamily
Ai Moriwins gold in bouldering and lead climbing at the IFSC World Cup in Morioka, Japan
LeBron and Bronny James become first father-son duo in NBA history to appear in a game together as LA Lakers beat Minnesota Timberwolves, 110-103 at Crypto.com Arena
US PresidentJohn F. Kennedymakes a live television address about Soviet missile bases in Cuba and imposes a naval blockade on Cuba, beginning the Cuban Missile Crisis
